name: Enoch Light
abstract: Enoch Henry Light (August 18, 1907 – July 31, 1978) was an American classically trained violinist, danceband leader, and recording engineer. As the leader of various dance bands that recorded as early as March 1927 and continuing through at least 1940, Light and his band primarily worked in various hotels in New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: And he was doing these recreations of band hits so called... they said he wants you to play “I Can’t Get Started.”... I played the solo note for note.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998
